prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "反向弓步" (reverse lunge) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a reverse lunge: front leg (right) standing in place with foot flat and knee bent at 90 degrees, thigh parallel to the floor; rear leg (left) stepped back with knee bent and hovering just above the ground, ball of the rear foot on the ground; torso upright with neutral spine, hands on hips, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the front leg, plus quadriceps. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hip flexors of the rear leg and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the front leg; "腘绳肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the hamstring and quadriceps area; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the rear leg. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
